calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-12-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Final Account From The Public Administrator
Off Calendar Aug-19-2008 Off Calendar

Petition For Approval Of Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-29-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon in Pro Per. Richard Shearer for Catholic Charities. John Aspelin for Susann Amherd and Mirianne Schiman. Doris Linnenbach for German Ladies Society.

Ms. Linnenbach stated that German Ladies allowed Catholic Charities to act with the understanding that $100,000.00 was to be split between them. Mr. Shearer stated that the apportionment of fees for Catholic Charities and German Ladies needed to be determined. Ms. Gordon stated that there was nothing in the settlement regarding fees for German Ladies. Mr. Aspelin stated that he was hired to resolve the will contest. The Court signed the order approving the settlement and the order admitting the will. The matter was set for status on the final account from the Public Administrator for August 19, 2008.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case #284730 (Conservatorship of Susanna Hindginder).

Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon in Pro Per (Executor). John Aspelin for the Contestants. Deborah Fox for Andrea Leung. Michael Bassi for Catholic Charities.

Ms. Fox stated that 4 out 5 agreed to Andrea Leung being appointed and that she had filed an Ex Parte petition for a Special Administrator. Mr. Aspelin argued that Ms. Leung was not a nuetral party, that she witnessed the will and stated that he had no problem with her being the Conservator. Ms. Gordon stated that Ms. Leung had control of the estate and that she should be appointed. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: B. Mann.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-23-2008 Continued To May-07-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-01-2008 Continued To Apr-23-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN ASPELIN APPEARING FOR THE CONTESTANTS;
ATTY. MICHAEL B. BASSI APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT CATHOLIC CHARTIES;
ATTY. DEBORAH L. FOX APPEARING FOR THE CONSERVATOR.
ATTY. MARY JANE GORDON, PETITIONER IN PRO PER.

MR. ASPELIN REPORTED THAT A WILL CONTEST HAS BEEN FILED AND NOTICE WAS GIVEN EXCEPT THE NEPHEW IN AUSTRIA. THE COURT INQUIRED WHETHER THERE IS A NEED FOR SPECIAL ADMINISTRATOR.
MS. GORDON RESPONDED THERE IS NO ASSET IN THE CONSERVATORSHIP PROCEEDINGS AND AWAITING FOR THE RESULT IN THE PROBATE CASE. SHE ALSO STATED THAT THERE WILL BE A HEARING ON THE CONSERVATORSHIP PROCEEDING ON APRIL 25, 2008. MR. ASPELIN REPORTED THAT DISCOVERY WILL BE DONE IN 90 DAYS. MR. BASSI STATED THAT HE HAS NOT SEEN ANY PAPER OR DOCUMENTS AND HE MIGHT OBJECT PENDING HEARING ON THE ACCOUNTING IN THE CONSERVATORSHIP CASE ON APRIL 25, 2008. THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. THE COURT WILL NOTIFY THE PARTIES OF THE RULING.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez, CSR 7782. Clerk: Cynthia Herbert. Bailiff: Deputy H. Harris.
